question: Describe the process of performing an in-order traversal on a binary tree and provide the sequence of nodes visited for a given small tree.
expectedPoints: Start traversal from the left subtree (if exists)

question: Explain the algorithm for inserting a new node into a Binary Search Tree (BST). How does this operation maintain the BST property, and what is its average time complexity?
expectedPoints: Start at the root and compare the new key with the current node
Recursively traverse left if key is smaller, right if larger
Insert the new node as a leaf when a null pointer is found
The operation preserves the BST property (left < root < right)
Average time complexity is O(log n) for a balanced tree

question: Compare and contrast the recursive and iterative approaches for performing a pre-order traversal on a binary tree. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of each method, including considerations for space complexity and stack overflow.
expectedPoints: Recursive method: simpler code, implicitly uses the call stack
Iterative method: uses an explicit stack data structure, more complex code
Space complexity for both is O(h) where h is the tree height
Risk of stack overflow for very deep trees in the recursive approach
Iterative approach offers more control and avoids recursion limits
Both methods result in the same traversal order (root, left, right)
